title: AI Engineer
company_focus: Day access marketplace for luxury hotel experiences
description:
About the company:

ResortPass is redefining what it means to be a hotel guest.
We’re building a new category in hospitality: day access to
luxury hotel experiences from breathtaking pools and private
beaches to deluxe spas and wellness recharges so people can
escape without an overnight stay.

Our marketplace now connects guests with 2,600+ partner
hotels and resorts, including Ritz-Carlton, Four Seasons,
Marriott Hilton, and more.  We’ve helped millions of people
enjoy premium hospitality close to home, making rest more
mindful, togetherness more meaningful, and escape more
accessible.  The result: unforgettable experiences for
guests and new revenue streams for partners.

Backed by some of the best consumer and growth investors,
including CRV, Declaration, Brian Kelly (The Points Guy),
WME, FJ Labs, and more, ResortPass is scaling a product
people love and creating an entirely new way to experience
hospitality.

About the role:

ResortPass is looking for an AI Engineer to sit at the
intersection of AI product development, data engineering,
and ML infrastructure. You'll be responsible for building
and deploying AI agents that automate real guest and partner
workflows, while also owning the ML infrastructure that
makes those systems reliable, observable, and smart.

Here are a few of the AI-powered products you'd be working
on:

- AI Concierge — A post-booking assistant that helps guests
  with day-of logistics, answers questions about their
  upcoming experience, and surfaces personalized upsell
  opportunities, all via SMS and chat.
- Voice AI Booking Assistant — An AI agent that calls hotel
  spas and front desks to book appointments on behalf of
  guests, handling hold times, retry logic, and multi-turn
  conversations with hotel staff.
- Conversational Search — A natural language search experience
  that helps guests discover the right hotel day pass based
  on what they're looking for ("a pool day near me with
  cabanas and cocktails"), rather than relying on filters
  alone.

This isn't a research role. You'll ship production AI
systems that integrate directly into our Rails backend and
interact with real guests, hotel partners, and third-party
booking platforms. You'll also build the real-time data
pipelines and model-serving infrastructure that power our
ML-driven marketplace — from search ranking to pricing to
personalization.

We're looking for someone who can move fluidly between
prompt engineering and pipeline engineering, someone equally
comfortable designing an agent orchestration framework as
they are debugging a dbt model or optimizing a Redshift
query. You'll work closely with our Product Engineering,
Data, and Business teams to turn AI from a prototype into a
core part of how ResortPass operates.

This role will be in person at our energetic NYC
headquarters.

The compensation for this role will range from
$180,000–$220,000 per year, plus equity, commensurate with
experience.

What you'll do

Primary focus: AI agents & real-time infrastructure

In your first 3–6 months, you'll ship production AI systems
and build the infrastructure to support them:

- Design, build, and iterate on AI agents that handle complex,
  multi-step workflows — automated booking, guest
  communication via SMS and voice, and partner integrations
  — taking them from prototype to production.
- Build and maintain the real-time and batch data
  infrastructure (pipelines, feature stores, model-serving
  layers) that supports our ML models and AI systems.
- Develop evaluation and observability frameworks for AI
  agents — including logging, human-in-the-loop feedback
  loops, and quality metrics — so we can measure and improve
  agent performance over time.

Secondary focus: ML lifecycle & backend integration

- Integrate AI capabilities directly into our Ruby on Rails
  backend, working alongside backend engineers to ensure
  agents and models are deployed reliably within our
  existing architecture.
- Own the full lifecycle of ML models in production: data
  collection, training, evaluation, deployment, monitoring,
  and iteration.
- Collaborate with Product and Business teams to identify
  high-impact opportunities for AI and translate them into
  well-scoped technical projects.
- Help shape ResortPass's AI strategy and technical roadmap as
  one of the earliest dedicated AI hires.

Your experience

Our current stack: Python (AI/ML systems), Ruby on Rails
(backend), dbt + Redshift (data warehouse &
transformations), AWS (ECS, Lambda), Datadog
(observability), Retell AI (voice agents), and Algolia
(search). You'll work across this stack and help us build
the next layer of AI/ML tooling on top of it.

We encourage candidates to apply even if they don't have
100% of the below qualifications. We believe in a holistic
approach when evaluating talent for our team and post new
roles often, so even if this role isn't quite right, we want
to meet you!

- 5+ years of experience in software engineering, data
  engineering, or ML engineering, with meaningful exposure
  to at least two of these areas.
- Hands-on experience building LLM-based applications or AI
  agents in production (not just notebooks) — including
  prompt design, tool use, orchestration, and evaluation.
- Strong Python skills and experience with ML/AI tooling
  (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, or similar orchestration
  frameworks; PyTorch or equivalent for model work).
- Solid data engineering fundamentals: building and
  maintaining ETL/ELT pipelines, working with data
  warehouses (e.g., Redshift) and data transformation tools
  like dbt.
- Experience deploying ML models or AI systems into production
  backend services, ideally in a web application context.
- Comfort working in or alongside a Ruby on Rails codebase —
  you don't need to be a Rails expert, but you should be
  able to integrate your work into an existing backend.
- Strong SQL skills and an intuition for data modeling and
  data quality.
- Demonstrated ability to work cross-functionally with
  product, engineering, and business stakeholders.

Nice to have

- Experience with real-time data infrastructure (streaming,
  event-driven architectures, feature stores).
- Experience with search and ranking systems (Algolia,
  Elasticsearch, or custom ML-based ranking).
- Background in marketplace or hospitality domains.
- Experience with AWS infrastructure (ECS, Lambda, SageMaker,
  or similar).
- Exposure to Datadog or similar observability platforms for
  monitoring AI/ML systems in production.

Perks & benefits

- Health, Dental & Vision — We're deeply invested in the
  health and well-being of our team and are proud to
  contribute to the monthly premiums of these insurance
  plans.
- Stock Option Plan — We offer employees the opportunity to
  become part-owners in our mission. Let's redefine what it
  means to be a guest, together.
- 401k plan — Save for your future with a 401k plan offering.
- Unlimited Paid Time Off (PTO) — Enjoy life away from work to
  be inspired and fully recharge with unlimited paid time
  off.
- Paid parental leave
- Commuter benefits
- Annual ResortPass credit — Our mission is to bring delight
  and relaxation to people around the world; including our
  employees! Access private beaches, deluxe saunas, awesome
  pools, and much more with your annual credit.
responsibilities: Design, build, and iterate on AI agents that handle complex, multi-step workflows including automated booking, guest communication via SMS and voice, and partner integrations
Build and maintain real-time and batch data infrastructure including pipelines, feature stores, and model-serving layers for ML models and AI systems
Develop evaluation and observability frameworks for AI agents including logging, human-in-the-loop feedback loops, and quality metrics
Integrate AI capabilities directly into Ruby on Rails backend, ensuring agents and models are deployed reliably
Own the full lifecycle of ML models in production: data collection, training, evaluation, deployment, monitoring, and iteration
Collaborate with Product and Business teams to identify high-impact AI opportunities and translate them into technical projects
Help shape AI strategy and technical roadmap as one of the earliest dedicated AI hires
use_cases: AI Concierge for post-booking guest assistance via SMS and chat with personalized upsell opportunities
Voice AI Booking Assistant that calls hotel spas and front desks to book appointments on behalf of guests
Conversational natural language search for discovering hotel day passes based on guest preferences
Search ranking, pricing optimization, and personalization for the hotel day pass marketplace
Automated guest and partner workflow automation
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves building, deploying, and iterating on AI
agents and LLM-based applications in production, including
agent orchestration, prompt engineering, and model serving.
The engineer will own the full ML model lifecycle and
directly build production AI systems like AI concierges and
voice booking assistants.
